segments.text: Now when he had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people, he entered into Capernaum.
And a certain centurion's servant, who was dear unto him, was sick, and ready to die.
And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.
And when they came to Jesus, they besought him instantly,
That he was worthy for whom he should do this
segments.speaker: Elders of the Jews

segments.text: For he loveth our nation, and he hath built us a synagogue.
Then Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him,
Lord, trouble not thyself: for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof
segments.speaker: Centurion's Friends

segments.text: Wherefore neither thought I myself worthy to come unto thee: but say in a word, and my servant shall be healed
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: For I also am a man set under authority, having under me soldiers, and I say unto one, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about,
said unto the people that followed him
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they that were sent, returning to the house, found the servant whole that had been sick.
And it came to pass the day after, that he went into a city called Nain; and many of his disciples went with him, and much people.
Now when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold, there was a dead man carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow: and much people of the city was with her.
And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her,
said unto her
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he came and touched the bier: and they that bare him stood still.
he said
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he that was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he delivered him to his mother.
And there came a fear on all:
That a great prophet is risen up among us; and, That God hath visited his people
segments.speaker: People

segments.text: And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisee's house, and sat down to meat.

heading: Jesus Anointed by a Sinful Woman

segments.text: And, behold, a woman in the city, which was a sinner, when she knew that Jesus sat at meat in the Pharisee's house, brought an alabaster box of ointment
And stood at his feet behind him weeping, and began to wash his feet with tears, and did wipe them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.
Now when the Pharisee which had bidden him saw it,
he spake within himself, saying, This man, if he were a prophet, would have known who and what manner of woman this is that toucheth him: for she is a sinner
segments.speaker: Simon the Pharisee

segments.text: And Jesus answering said unto him,
Master, say on
segments.speaker: Simon

segments.text: Who is this that forgiveth sins also?
segments.speaker: Those at the meal

segments.text: he said to the woman
segments.speaker: Jesus
